root_cause.text: Spring Data JPA appended user-provided `Sort` expressions directly to the query string's `ORDER BY` clause without validation, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary JPQL functions or expressions.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces `checkSortExpression(Order order)` in `QueryUtils.java`, which uses a regular expression (`PUNCTATION_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(".*((?![\\._])[\\p{Punct}|\\s])")`) to reject sort properties containing punctuation or spaces, throwing an `InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException` unless the expression is explicitly marked as safe using the newly introduced `JpaSort.unsafe()`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: Spring Data JPA appended user-provided `Sort` expressions directly to the query string's `ORDER BY` clause without validation, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary JPQL functions or expressions.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces `checkSortExpression(Order order)` in `QueryUtils.java`, which uses a regular expression (`PUNCTATION_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(".*((?![\\._])[\\p{Punct}|\\s])")`) to reject sort properties containing punctuation or spaces, throwing an `InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException` unless the expression is explicitly marked as safe using the newly introduced `JpaSort.unsafe()`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add documentation for using Sort and JpaSort.unsafe with JPA query methods.
intent_record.what: Added a new asciidoc section explaining how to use Sort with query methods.
intent_record.how: Provides documentation and code examples clarifying the use of standard sorting and explicitly unsafe sorting via JpaSort.unsafe.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year to 2016.
intent_record.what: The copyright year in the file header was changed from 2015 to 2016.
intent_record.how: This change has no effect on the runtime behavior or functionality of the code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update class documentation to include an additional author.
intent_record.what: Added an @author tag for Christoph Strobl to the JavaDoc comment of the JpaSort class.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior, as it only updates documentation metadata.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a private constructor to JpaSort for instantiating with a list of orders.
intent_record.what: Added a private constructor that takes a List of Order objects.
intent_record.how: Provides an internal way to instantiate JpaSort using an existing list of orders, likely to support methods that combine or build upon existing sort instances.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add `andUnsafe` method to allow combining existing sorts with new string-based sorting properties.
intent_record.what: Added a new method `andUnsafe` to the `JpaSort` class.
intent_record.how: Provides functionality to append new string-based properties with a specified sorting direction to the existing sorting criteria, returning a new `JpaSort` instance.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add `unsafe` static factory methods to `JpaSort` to enable sorting by raw string properties.
intent_record.what: Add three overloaded static factory methods named `unsafe` to the `JpaSort` class that accept raw string properties and an optional sorting direction.
intent_record.how: Provides a way to instantiate a `JpaSort` object using raw string properties without strictly typed attribute paths, bypassing the usual compile-time safety checks.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add JpaOrder class to JpaSort to support unsafe sorting configurations.
intent_record.what: Added a new nested static class JpaOrder extending Order inside JpaSort with support for an unsafe flag and various builder methods.
intent_record.how: Allows developers to create JpaOrder instances that track whether a sorting property is 'unsafe', integrating with Spring Data's sorting mechanism while maintaining null handling and ignore-case configurations.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports for Spring Data JPA sorting and exception handling.
intent_record.what: Added two import statements for InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException and JpaOrder.
intent_record.how: Makes the exception and sorting classes available to the rest of the file's code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add author to the class Javadoc.
intent_record.what: Added an author tag to the Javadoc comment.
intent_record.how: This change updates documentation metadata without affecting any runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add new regex patterns and constants to support enhanced function and alias parsing in queries.
intent_record.what: Added static final constants for a punctuation regex pattern, a function alias group name, and an uninitialized function pattern.
intent_record.how: Introduces new regex patterns and string constants that will be used in the subsequent static block and query parsing logic to better extract aliases or handle punctuation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a regex pattern to detect and extract function aliases in queries.
intent_record.what: Added a new regular expression pattern named FUNCTION_PATTERN to match SQL function calls and their aliases.
intent_record.how: Compiles a regex that identifies function calls with an 'AS' alias, enabling the system to extract the alias name during query parsing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Include function aliases in order clause generation to support sorting by function results.
intent_record.what: Added extraction of function aliases and modified the getOrderClause call to include them.
intent_record.how: Provides the order clause generation logic with function aliases, enabling proper sorting on functional expressions.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix order clause generation to validate sort expressions and correctly handle function aliases.
intent_record.what: Added a functionAlias parameter, a checkSortExpression validation call, and an early return for known function aliases.
intent_record.how: Validates sort expressions to prevent invalid queries and correctly formats the order clause when the ordered property is a recognized function alias.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add getFunctionAliases method to extract aggregate function aliases from queries.
intent_record.what: Added a new static method getFunctionAliases to extract function aliases from a query string.
intent_record.how: Uses a regex matcher to find and return a set of aliases used for aggregate functions within the provided query.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add validation to prevent unsafe sort expressions containing functions or expressions.
intent_record.what: Added a new method checkSortExpression.
intent_record.how: Throws an exception if a sort order property contains forbidden punctuation, unless it is explicitly marked as unsafe.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year to 2016
intent_record.what: Updated the copyright year in the header comment.
intent_record.how: No functional changes occur at runtime.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import JpaOrder to make it available for use in the file.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for org.springframework.data.jpa.domain.JpaSort.JpaOrder.
intent_record.how: Makes the JpaOrder class available in the file's namespace, enabling its use in subsequent code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add Christoph Strobl as an author in the Javadoc.
intent_record.what: Added an @author tag for Christoph Strobl to the class Javadoc.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on program behavior as it only updates documentation metadata.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests for creating and combining unsafe JpaSort objects.
intent_record.what: Added four new test methods to JpaSortTests to verify the behavior of unsafe sorts and their combinations with safe sorts.
intent_record.how: Provides test coverage for the JpaSort.unsafe() method and the andUnsafe() / and() combinators to ensure they produce the correct Order instances.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year to 2016.
intent_record.what: Updated the copyright year in the header comment from 2015 to 2016.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior as it only modifies a comment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add required imports for updated test implementation.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for Spring framework classes.
intent_record.how: Allows the test class to reference InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException and JpaSort directly.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add Christoph Strobl as an author to the class JavaDoc.
intent_record.what: An author tag for Christoph Strobl was added to the JavaDoc comment of the QueryUtilsUnitTests class.
intent_record.how: This change only affects documentation and has no functional impact on the runtime behavior of the code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test to expect InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException when attempting to prefix sorts with a function.
intent_record.what: Added an expected exception to the @Test annotation.
intent_record.how: The test will now pass only if it throws an InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException, asserting the correct error handling behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests to verify sorting behavior with aliases, function calls, and whitespace validation in JPA queries.
intent_record.what: Added multiple new unit tests to QueryUtilsUnitTests to test various sorting edge cases.
intent_record.how: Verifies the behavior of applySorting when encountering sort properties with whitespace, unsafe sorts, and aliased function calls (including those with parameters, underscores, dots, or extra whitespaces).

grouping_result.intent: Add documentation for using Sort and JpaSort.unsafe with JPA query methods.
Add Christoph Strobl as an author to the class JavaDoc.
Add a private constructor to JpaSort for instantiating with a list of orders.
Add unit tests for creating and combining unsafe JpaSort objects.
Add necessary imports for Spring Data JPA sorting and exception handling.
Add getFunctionAliases method to extract aggregate function aliases from queries.
Update test to expect InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException when attempting to prefix sorts with a function.
Add validation to prevent unsafe sort expressions containing functions or expressions.
Add unit tests to verify sorting behavior with aliases, function calls, and whitespace validation in JPA queries.

consistency_review.core_intent: Update copyright year to 2016 across various files.
Add an author (Christoph Strobl) to the class Javadoc.
Add unsafe sorting support to JpaSort, including factory methods, combination methods, and corresponding unit tests.
Add regular expressions and methods to parse and extract function aliases from queries.
Fix order clause generation to properly handle and validate function aliases in sort expressions, and update related tests.
Add unit tests and required imports to verify sorting behavior with aliases, function calls, and whitespace validation in JPA queries.

relevance_review.reason: The group only adds documentation for using Sort and JpaSort.unsafe, which does not contain the actual code changes to fix the vulnerability root cause.
The changes only update the copyright year and do not contain any logic related to the vulnerability.
The group only updates Javadoc to add an author, which is unrelated to validating Sort expressions or fixing the injection vulnerability.

Adding a private constructor to JpaSort is a structural/refactoring change that does not directly address the missing validation or escaping of Sort expressions causing the JPQL injection vulnerability.

The group introduces explicit 'unsafe' sorting methods and classes (JpaSort.unsafe), which is the mechanism used to restrict arbitrary JPQL expressions in default sorting and force explicit opt-in, directly addressing the injection vulnerability.
Adding imports for sorting and exception handling is directly required to implement the validation logic that addresses the unvalidated Sort expressions vulnerability.

Adding regex patterns and methods to parse and extract function aliases is part of the mechanism introduced to properly validate and handle functions in queries, directly addressing the root cause of arbitrary JPQL function injection.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding validation to sort expressions and restricting the use of functions in the order clause, preventing the injection of arbitrary JPQL functions.

The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding validation to prevent unsafe sort expressions containing functions or expressions, which prevents the JPQL injection described in the root cause.
The group adds unit tests that specifically verify the new sorting validation logic, including handling of aliases, function calls, and whitespace, which is the direct fix for the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line shows that the code historically expected and allowed function calls in sort properties without validating them, which led to the injection vulnerability.
This line directly appends the generated, potentially unsafe order clause into the query.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement explicitly allowed parentheses in sort properties to bypass reference qualification, enabling attackers to inject arbitrary JPQL functions into the ORDER BY clause.
This statement directly appends the generated order clause (which was not properly validated for unsafe expressions) into the query.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
DATAJPA-148 - Remove qualification of sort reference for functions.

When applying Sort instances we now check for the p
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 4ff0671105dac4bf94cbed7c3dcf12541ed1075f
Author: Oliver Gierke <ogierke@gopivotal.com>
Date:   Thu Jun 27 22:19:01 2013 +0200

    DATAJPA-148 - Remove qualification of sort reference for functions.
    
    When applying Sort instances we now check for the presence of a ( which indicates a function to be executed.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java b/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
index 4c
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit explicitly adds a check `!property.contains("(")` to avoid qualifying references that contain parentheses, which is intended to allow functions. This directly enables the injection of arbitrary JPQL functions via the Sort expressions, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
DATAJPA-252 - Applying sort to manually defined queries considers joins.

If a manually defined query uses outer joins,
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a9cfe73511342c7f7d95a591bbf5ca1e7a8c1e86
Author: Oliver Gierke <ogierke@vmware.com>
Date:   Wed Aug 29 17:16:33 2012 +0200

    DATAJPA-252 - Applying sort to manually defined queries considers joins.
    
    If a manually defined query uses outer joins, prefixing the sort criteria handed to the execution of the method causes an additional join being added as the JPA considers path expressions to be inner joins always (see Sect. 4.4.4, JPA 2.0 spec).
    
    We now parse the outer join
bic_trace_step.reason: The getOrderClause method retrieves the property from the Order object using order.getProperty() and directly formats it into the order by clause via String.format("%s%s %s, ", ..., property, ...). There is no validation to prevent arbitrary JPQL expressions from being injected into the ORDER BY clause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: DATAJPA-98 - Formatted sources with Spring Data Eclipse formatting settings.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b291a5f5f9928491567b3cb1b4a78d105c54af82
Author: Oliver Gierke <info@olivergierke.de>
Date:   Fri Sep 2 18:07:48 2011 +0200

    DATAJPA-98 - Formatted sources with Spring Data Eclipse formatting settings.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java b/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
index b1d385741..25bc6089e 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
+++
bic_trace_step.reason: The applySorting method iterates over the Sort Orders and appends `order.getProperty()` directly into the query string: `builder.append(String.format(" %s.%s %s,", alias, order.getProperty(), toJpaDirection(order)));`. This happens without any validation of the property, which matches the vulnerability description.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Initial commit of Spring JPA repository abstraction.

Port of the Hades project whereas the JPA independent part resides
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e8c5666070fc195dc6e9431a92013bff2ff926a0
Author: Oliver Gierke <info@olivergierke.de>
Date:   Sun Nov 7 18:47:38 2010 +0100

    Initial commit of Spring JPA repository abstraction.
    
    Port of the Hades project whereas the JPA independent part resides in Spring Data Commons Core. Reflect changes refactorings for DATACMNS-2, DATACMNS-3, DATACMNS-4, DATACMNS-5, DATACMNS-6, DATACMNS-8, DATACMNS-9, DATAJPA-2.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/Quer
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `applySorting` appends the `order.getProperty()` directly to the `order by` clause using `String.format(" %s.%s %s,", alias, order.getProperty(), toJpaDirection(order))` without validating or sanitizing `order.getProperty()`. This directly introduces the vulnerable construct described in the root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
DATAJPA-296 - Fixed application of ignore case flag on string queries.

The ignore-case flag held in a Sort.Order is now
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 4c65ad1df6ea281036b5d35c25473a57f0bc3ac0

    DATAJPA-296 - Fixed application of ignore case flag on string queries.
    
    The ignore-case flag held in a Sort.Order is now also applied when manually defining queries.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java b/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
index 8ab3a99ac..3a173b
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `getOrderClause` constructs the ORDER BY clause by directly formatting the property reference (which comes from the user-provided Sort.Order) into the query string, as seen in `String.format("%s %s", wrapped, toJpaDirection(order))`. There is no validation or sanitization applied to prevent injection of arbitrary JPQL expressions.

If a manually defined query uses outer joins,

    DATAJPA-252 - Applying sort to manually defined queries considers joins.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: DATAJPA-98 - Formatted sources with Spring Data Eclipse formatting settings.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b291a5f5f9928491567b3cb1b4a78d105c54af82
Author: Oliver Gierke <info@olivergierke.de>

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java b/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
index b1d385741..25bc6089e 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/springframework/data/jpa/repository/query/QueryUtils.java
+++
bic_trace_step.reason: The applySorting() method directly appends user-provided 'Sort' parameters (order.getProperty()) into the query string's 'order by' clause via String.format without validation or sanitization ('builder.append(String.format(" %s.%s %s,", alias, order.getProperty(), toJpaDirection(order)));'). This allows arbitrary JPQL injection, representing the vulnerability root cause. This commit only changes whitespace/formatting and does not add a fix.
